## Top-Level Files & Directories

| Path | Description |
|---|---|
| `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` | Rust workspace manifest and lockfile |
| `src/` | Main Rust source (runtime, server, providers, tools) |
| `sdk/` | Python & TypeScript SDKs |
| `examples/` | Example agents, prompts, tools, and SDK demo |
| `sandbox-js/` | WASM sandbox for running AI-generated JavaScript |
| `sandbox-python/` | WASM sandbox for running AI-generated Python |
| `sandbox-runtime/` | Shared WASM sandbox runtime |
| `scripts/` | Build scripts (e.g., WASM compilation) |
| `docs/` | Additional documentation |
| `DESIGN.md` | Full architecture & design rationale |
| `README.md` | Project overview and quick start |
| `TODO.md` | Implementation roadmap |
| `llm.txt` | Complete API reference for LLM-assisted development |

## Summary

**App Agent Framework** is a YAML-free AI agent framework where agents are written as **Starlark** scripts (a deterministic, Python-dialect language). Its Rust core runtime executes `.star` agent files, providing a fixed set of host functions — `prompt()`, `tool()`, `http()`, `memory()`, `exec()`, and others — as the only interface to the outside world. Because Starlark is deterministic and all side effects are logged, the framework supports **zero-cost checkpointing and replay**: an agent's full execution can be saved to disk and re-run later with zero LLM API calls, enabling cheap debugging, deterministic testing, and crash recovery. The runtime can be used as a one-shot CLI (`app-agent run`) or as an HTTP server (`app-agent serve`) for event-driven/webhook agents, and is complemented by a pure-stdlib Python SDK (no `pip install` required) that communicates with the server over HTTP.
